metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `detector_matches` to `rebar.llm.criteria.__init__.py` imports and `__all__` so the promoted symbol is reachable at the package level, consistent with the plan's stated goal of publishing it as a public API.
findings.finding: `rebar.llm.criteria.__init__.py` is a missing edit target: it does not export `_detector_matches` today, and the plan does not mention adding `detector_matches` to its `__all__` / public surface after the rename. Any caller reaching the symbol via the package namespace (`from rebar.llm.criteria import detector_matches`) will get an `ImportError` unless the `__init__.py` is also updated.
findings.suggested_fix: In `src/rebar/llm/criteria/__init__.py`, add `detector_matches` to the `from .model import (...)` block and to `__all__`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for whether `detector_matches` should (or deliberately should not) be re-exported from `rebar.llm.criteria.__init__.__all__`, and update `__init__.py` accordingly if it should be part of the package's public surface.
findings.finding: The plan promotes `_detector_matches` to a public name in `criteria.model` but never states whether `rebar.llm.criteria` (the package's public `__init__`) should also export it. The `criteria/__init__.__all__` currently lists `build_descriptor`, `threshold_for`, `CriteriaError`, etc., but not `detector_matches`. A future cross-package consumer using the package surface (`from rebar.llm.criteria import detector_matches`) would find nothing — the plan's chosen approach (submodule-direct import) is defensible but the positive rationale for NOT adding it to the package surface is absent, leaving the interface-boundary decision implicit.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `detector_matches` to `criteria/__init__.py`'s imports and `__all__` alongside `build_descriptor` — since `detector_matches` is the selector primitive that external DET consumers (both gates) need, it belongs on the same package surface as `build_descriptor`. If it is intentionally kept at the submodule level only, the plan should state that rationale explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default assumption explicitly: either enumerate all known importers (currently only `det_invariants.py`) or add a discovery command (e.g. `grep -rn 'registry.*_detector_matches\|from .registry import _detector_matches' src/`) so the executor has a bounded scope.
findings.finding: The cascade_flag says 'sweep importers of `registry._detector_matches`' without enumerating them, leaving the scope of the rename open-ended. An executor must independently discover all import sites, creating an unstated investigation step.
findings.location: cascade_flag note: 'rename + re-export removal — sweep importers of `registry._detector_matches`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the cascade_flag line with: 'cascade_flag: rename + re-export removal — run `grep -rn "_detector_matches" src/` to enumerate all importers; currently known: `det_invariants.py` only. Update every hit before deleting the shim.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: e.g. `grep -rn 'def detector_matches' src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py` for AC1; `grep -rn '_detector_matches' src/` returning zero results for AC2; and a named test command such as `pytest tests/llm/plan_review/ -q` for AC3.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for any of the three acceptance criteria. AC1 ('detector_matches is public in criteria.model') and AC2 ('noqa re-export removed; direct import in place') have no grep/inspection commands, and AC3 ('tests green') does not name the test suite or command to run, leaving done-ness unverifiable from the plan text.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to each AC a parenthetical proving command:
• AC1: `python -c 'from rebar.llm.criteria.model import detector_matches'` exits 0.
• AC2: `grep -rn '_detector_matches\|noqa.*F401' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` returns no matches.
• AC3: `pytest tests/ -k det_invariant -q` exits 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies no remaining import of `registry._detector_matches` (or `_detector_matches`) exists anywhere outside `criteria.model` after the migration (e.g., a grep/AST check or a new test that would fail on stale imports).
findings.finding: The cascade sweep of all importers of `registry._detector_matches` is described as required work in the remediation section but has no dedicated acceptance criterion. AC3 ('tests green') is only an indirect proxy and would miss importers in untested code paths.
findings.location: Remediation section: 'cascade_flag: rename + re-export removal — sweep importers of `registry._detector_matches`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'A project-wide grep (or import-linting rule) confirms zero remaining references to `_detector_matches` outside `criteria.model`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit expand-contract sequence (step 1: add `detector_matches` public name; step 2: confirm all importers updated; step 3: delete `_detector_matches` shim) and/or a rollback note stating how to revert if a missed importer causes a breakage.
findings.finding: The plan removes the `registry.py` re-export shim (a de-facto cross-package interface) without providing an expand-contract sequence or rollback path, despite the cascade_flag warning of unknown additional importers.
findings.suggested_fix: Split into two commits/steps: (1) add `detector_matches` as an alias alongside `_detector_matches` in `criteria.model`; (2) after confirming all importers swept and tests green, delete `_detector_matches` and the `registry.py` shim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that confirms no file in the repo still imports `_detector_matches` (private name) from any module — e.g., a grep/test assertion — so the cascade sweep is end-state-observable.
findings.finding: The cascade_flag calls for sweeping all importers of `registry._detector_matches` (and the old private `criteria.model._detector_matches`), but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that sweep is complete — leaving possible stale private-name imports undetected after the rename.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] `grep -r "_detector_matches"` returns no matches outside of the definition site in `criteria.model` (or the name is gone entirely).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
